questions.question: How would you describe yourself to someone who has never met you?
What is something about you that most people do not know?
What are three words your friends would use to describe you?
What makes you different from everyone else you know?
What part of your personality are you most proud of?
How have you changed in the last year?
What does a real friendship look like to you?
Who is someone you can completely be yourself around?
Have you ever lost a friend? What happened?
What is the best thing a friend has ever done for you?
How do you know when someone is a true friend versus just being nice?
What do you bring to a friendship that is unique?
What is the best thing about your school experience right now?
Has a teacher ever changed the way you think about something?
What class or subject lights you up inside?
What is the most stressful part of school for you?
If you could redesign school, what would you change?
What is something you learned in school that actually stuck with you?
What do you dream about doing with your life?
If money was not an issue, what would you spend your life doing?
What kind of life do you want to build for yourself?
Who is someone you look up to and why?
What is something you are working toward right now?
Where do you see yourself in five years?
What is your relationship like with your parents right now?
What is the best advice someone in your family ever gave you?
What do you wish your family understood about you?
Who in your family do you feel the closest to?
How are you similar to your parents, and how are you different?
What family tradition means the most to you?
What puts the most pressure on you right now?
How do you handle stress when everything feels like too much?
Do you feel like people expect too much from you sometimes?
What is something you do to decompress and just be yourself?
Have you ever felt burned out? What did you do about it?
Who do you talk to when things feel heavy?
How does social media make you feel about yourself, honestly?
Do you think people are more real or more fake online?
Has the internet ever changed your mind about something important?
What would your life look like without your phone for a week?
Do you compare yourself to people online? How do you deal with that?
What is the best thing technology has given your generation?
What do you believe in more than anything?
Where do your values come from — family, friends, or your own experience?
Is there something you stand for that not everyone agrees with?
What does being a good person mean to you?
Has your belief system changed as you have gotten older?
What is a line you would never cross, no matter what?
What is the biggest mistake you have learned from?
Have you ever failed at something that really mattered to you?
How do you deal with failure — do you shut down or push through?
What is something you tried and it did not work out, but you are glad you tried?
What did your hardest moment teach you about yourself?
Do you think failure is necessary for growth?
What does a healthy relationship look like to you?
Have you ever had your heart broken? How did you get through it?
What do you think is the most important quality in a partner?
How do you know the difference between love and infatuation?
What relationship advice would you give to your younger self?
What have your relationships taught you about yourself?
What does a good mental health day look like for you?
Do you think your generation talks about mental health enough?
What is something that helps your mental health that might surprise people?
When was the last time you felt truly at peace?
How do you tell the difference between a bad day and something deeper?
What would you want someone to say to you on your worst day?
What is something you could talk about for hours?
When do you feel the most alive?
What hobby or interest do you wish you had more time for?
Is there something you love that other people think is weird?
How did you discover the thing you are most passionate about?
If you could turn any passion into a career, what would it be?
What is the most grown-up decision you have had to make?
When did you first feel like you were becoming your own person?
What is something you figured out completely on your own?
Are you excited or nervous about being more independent?
What is the first big thing you want to do on your own?
What does freedom mean to you at this point in your life?
Have you ever done something just because everyone else was doing it?
How do you say no when you really do not want to do something?
What is the bravest thing you have done by going against the crowd?
Do you think it is harder to fit in or stand out?
How do you decide what is right when everyone around you disagrees?
What would you tell someone who feels pressured to be someone they are not?
What song or album has changed your life?
What kind of music do you listen to when you are alone?
Has a movie, show, or book ever made you see the world differently?
What does your generation's culture look like to you?
Is there an artist or creator who speaks to exactly how you feel?
What would be on the soundtrack of your life right now?
What was your first job or how do you earn money?
What have you learned about money that nobody taught you in school?
Do you think your generation will have it harder or easier financially?
Would you rather have a job you love that pays less or a job you hate that pays a lot?
What is the smartest thing you have done with money so far?
What does success look like to you — is it money, happiness, or something else?
What does taking care of yourself mean to you?
How do you feel about your body right now — honestly?
What is one healthy habit you are proud of?
Do you think society puts too much pressure on how people look?
What makes you feel strong, physically or mentally?
What is something you do for your health that actually makes you feel good?
How do you express yourself — through words, music, art, sports, or something else?
When do you feel most creative?
Is there something you have created that you are really proud of?
Do you journal, write, or keep any kind of record of your thoughts?
What would you create if you knew nobody would judge you?
How does creating something make you feel?
What is one thing about the world right now that frustrates you?
What gives you hope for the future?
Do you think your generation will change the world? How?
What issue do you care most deeply about?
If you could sit down with any world leader, what would you say?
What does justice mean to you?
What do you miss most about being a little kid?
Is there a memory from childhood that still makes you smile?
What toy, game, or show from when you were little do you still think about?
When did you first realize you were not a little kid anymore?
What is something from your childhood you wish still existed?
If you could go back and tell your 8-year-old self one thing, what would it be?
What does trust mean to you?
Has anyone ever broken your trust? How did you handle it?
How do you earn someone's trust?
Who is the most trustworthy person in your life?
Do you forgive easily or does it take time?
What is the difference between loyalty and being a pushover?
Have you ever felt really alone even in a room full of people?
Where do you feel like you truly belong?
What does it feel like when you find your people?
How do you deal with loneliness?
Do you think being alone and being lonely are different things?
What group, place, or person makes you feel like you are home?
What are you most grateful for right now?
Who is someone you have never properly thanked?
What is a small thing that makes your day better?
What is something you take for granted that you probably should not?
When was the last time something really good happened to you?
What would you miss most if it was suddenly gone?
What is the scariest thing you have ever done on purpose?
What risk are you glad you took?
What is something you want to do but are afraid to try?
When was a time you stood up for yourself?
Do you think courage is the absence of fear or doing something despite fear?
What would you do if you were guaranteed not to fail?
Do you feel like you have a purpose? What is it?
What makes you feel like what you do matters?
How do you think you will make a difference in the world?
What is something bigger than yourself that you care about?
When do you feel most useful or needed?
What would you want your legacy to be?
What is the biggest change you have gone through?
How do you handle change — do you fight it or go with it?
What is something that changed you as a person?
Is there a version of yourself from the past that you barely recognize?
What change are you going through right now?
What is one thing you would never want to change about your life?
What does home feel like to you?
Where did you grow up and what was it like?
Do you want to stay close to home or get as far away as possible?
What is your favorite memory of your hometown?
Is home a place, a person, or a feeling for you?
What will you take with you from where you grew up, no matter where you go?
What do you think love actually is?
What is the most important thing in a romantic relationship?
How do you know when someone genuinely cares about you?
What is one red flag you will never ignore?
What is the best relationship advice you have ever received?
Do you believe in soulmates or do you think love is something you build?
When do you feel the most confident?
What is something you are insecure about that you wish you were not?
How do you build yourself back up after being knocked down?
Who makes you feel like the best version of yourself?
What is one thing you have learned to accept about yourself?
What does real confidence look like to you — not cockiness, real confidence?
What do you want people to say about you when you are not in the room?
If you could leave one message for the world, what would it be?
What is the most important thing you have learned so far in life?
Who has had the biggest impact on who you are today?
What do you want your life to stand for?
If someone listened to all your stories 50 years from now, what would they learn about you?
What does your cultural background mean to you?
Have you ever felt like you did not fit in because of where you come from?
What tradition from your culture do you want to keep alive?
What have you learned from someone whose background is different from yours?
How does your heritage shape who you are?
What do you wish people understood about your culture?
Where do you go when you need to be alone?
Do you enjoy being in nature? What does it do for you?
What is the most beautiful place you have ever been?
Do you need alone time to recharge or do you get energy from people?
What sounds make you feel calm?
When was the last time you felt completely at peace with everything?
What makes you really angry?
How do you handle conflict — do you confront it or avoid it?
Have you ever said something in anger you wish you could take back?
What is the best way someone has resolved a fight with you?
Do you think anger is always bad or can it be useful?
What have arguments taught you about yourself?
Do you consider yourself a spiritual person?
What do you think happens when we die?
Is there something bigger than us out there?
What gives your life meaning?
Have you ever had a moment that felt bigger than you can explain?
Where do you find peace when life gets chaotic?
What are you most excited about for your next chapter?
What scares you about the future?
Do you think college is necessary or are there other paths?
What would you study if grades and expectations did not matter?
What is the biggest decision you are facing right now?
How do you make big decisions — gut feeling, logic, or asking for advice?
What makes you laugh harder than anything?
Who is the funniest person you know?
What is the most embarrassing thing that happened to you that you can laugh about now?
What is your go-to thing when you need to feel happy?
Do you use humor to cope with things? How?
What is the funniest memory you have?
What does your morning routine look like?
What is one habit you wish you could start?
What is one habit you wish you could break?
Are you a night owl or an early bird?
What is something small you do every day that makes your life better?
How do you spend your time when nobody is watching?
Who has influenced you the most outside of your family?
Is there a teacher, coach, or mentor who believed in you when you needed it?
What is the most important thing someone older taught you?
Have you ever been a mentor to someone younger?
What kind of role model do you want to be?
If you could apprentice under anyone in the world, who would it be?
Do you have any regrets?
Is there something you wish you had said to someone but never did?
What does forgiveness mean to you — is it for them or for you?
Have you ever had to forgive yourself for something?
What is something you would do differently if you could go back?
Do you believe people can truly change?
Where is the most interesting place you have ever been?
Where in the world do you most want to go?
Do you prefer adventures or relaxing vacations?
What is the best trip you have ever taken?
Would you rather travel the world or build a life in one place?
What did traveling teach you that staying home never could?

Do you ever feel like you are wearing a mask around certain people?
What is something about the real you that you hide from most people?
When did you stop caring what everyone thinks?
What does being authentic actually mean to you?
Who sees the real you — the unfiltered version?
Are you competitive? About what?
How do you handle losing?
What drives you more — wanting to win or being afraid to fail?
Do you compare yourself to other people? How do you deal with that?
What is your biggest personal victory so far?
Is ambition a good thing or can it go too far?
What is the most important thing you have learned so far?
What is something you know now that you wish you knew two years ago?
What has life taught you about people?
What is the hardest truth you have had to accept?
If you could write a life rule for yourself, what would it be?
What is a lesson you had to learn the hard way?
What makes your generation different from your parents' generation?
What do older people get wrong about your age group?
What is the best thing about growing up right now?
What is the hardest thing about being your age in this era?
What do you think history will say about your generation?
What do you want to fix that the older generations could not?
What is the hardest thing you have ever had to tell someone?
Do you find it easy or hard to be vulnerable?
When was the last time you cried and why?
What is something you need but have a hard time asking for?
Do you think being vulnerable is a strength or a weakness?
What would change if you let people see the real you more often?
What is something you have accomplished that you are really proud of?
Do you celebrate your wins or just move on to the next thing?
What is the best compliment anyone has ever given you?
When was the last time you felt truly proud of yourself?
What is something good happening in your life right now?
If you could brag about one thing without being judged, what would it be?
What does your dream life look like 10 years from now?
What are you most looking forward to in the next few years?
What kind of person do you want to become?
What is one thing you promise yourself you will never stop doing?
If future you could see you right now, what would they think?
What do you want to be remembered for when your story is told?
What is your comfort food?
Can you cook? What is your signature dish?
What meal reminds you of home?
If you could have dinner with anyone, dead or alive, who and what would you eat?
What food from your childhood do you still crave?
Do you eat to live or live to eat?
Do you feel like time is moving fast or slow right now?
What moment in your life do you wish you could freeze?
If you had an extra hour every day, how would you spend it?
What is something you wish you had started earlier?
Do you think about the past more or the future more?
What would you do today if you knew tomorrow was not guaranteed?
If you could write a letter to your future self, what would you say?
What do you want to remember about who you are right now?
What are you struggling with that you hope gets easier?
What is something you are proud of about the person you are becoming?
If someone found a recording of you 30 years from now, what would you want them to hear?
Say something right now that you want your future kids or grandkids to hear someday.
